title: Pollinator Scout
subtitle: Start with flowers, then watch who visits
learning_goal: See pollination as an interaction between plants and flower visitors.
intro: Flowers are meeting places. Find a bloom, then watch for the insects that move between them.
outro: You connected a plant to its visitors, which is the beginning of pollinator fieldwork.

steps.hint: Get the flower in frame. Leaves alone are harder to identify.
Bees, butterflies, moths, flies, wasps, and ants all count here.
A second photo of the same species will not advance this step.
